The content of `src/greet.py` shows that the whole file content got written with literal `\n` characters instead of actual new lines. This will cause the import to break as the file is not valid Python syntax.

item.command:
/bin/bash -lc "echo '#"'!/usr/bin/env python3
import argparse

def greet(name: str) -> str:
    return f"Hello, {name}!"

def main():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Simple greeter CLI")
    parser.add_argument("name", help="Name to greet")
    args = parser.parse_args()
    greeting = greet(args.name)
    print(greeting)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()'"' > src/greet.py"

/bin/bash -lc "echo '#"'!/usr/bin/env python3
import argparse

def greet(name: str) -> str:
    return f"Hello, {name}!"

def main():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Simple greeter CLI")
    parser.add_argument("name", help="Name to greet")
    args = parser.parse_args()
    greeting = greet(args.name)
    print(greeting)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()'"' > src/greet.py"
